methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $310K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Orbital Robotics is a pre-seed stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Aaron Borger, Riley Mark, Sohil Pokharna, and Doug Kohl. The company has raised $310K to date. The valuation universe includes 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers across the sector, including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs.

Of three standard triangulation methods, only stage-progression proved applicable. The peer-median approach could not be executed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort; while 23 peers exist in the sector broadly, none met the minimum three-datapoint threshold for defensible comparison. Public-comp anchoring was inapplicable, as private companies do not typically disclose revenue figures needed for price-to-sales multiples. The Space & Aerospace public median P/S is 59.8x, but without Orbital Robotics' revenue data this cannot inform valuation.

Stage-progression applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage advancement against the $310K raised proxy, yielding $775K. Given reliance on a single method, the triangulated range is $659K (low) to $891K (high), with a median of $775K. Key uncertainties include product-market fit, technical feasibility of orbital robotics applications, and path to revenue. The narrow analytical foundation reflects early-stage opacity; future valuation iterations should prioritize revenue disclosure or identification of tighter peer comparables.
